Workaround: download `gradle-wrapper.jar` in CI

If you cannot commit binaries but need the Gradle wrapper to run, you can download the wrapper JAR during CI.

Example (bash):

```sh
set -euo pipefail
cd founder-academy-242883-242892/fouguide_frontend/android/gradle/wrapper

# Choose the same Gradle version as in gradle-wrapper.properties
GRADLE_VERSION="8.10.2"

# Download gradle distribution and extract wrapper jar
curl -fsSL "https://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-${GRADLE_VERSION}-bin.zip" -o gradle.zip
unzip -q gradle.zip "gradle-${GRADLE_VERSION}/lib/plugins/gradle-wrapper-main-${GRADLE_VERSION}.jar" -d /tmp/gradle_extracted
unzip -q gradle.zip "gradle-${GRADLE_VERSION}/lib/gradle-wrapper-shared-${GRADLE_VERSION}.jar" -d /tmp/gradle_extracted

# NOTE: Gradle 8 uses multiple jars; the wrapper expects a single gradle-wrapper.jar.
# The simplest supported approach remains: commit gradle-wrapper.jar generated by `gradle wrapper`.
#
# If your environment has `gradle` installed, prefer avoiding wrapper:
#   gradle -p ../../.. tasks
```

Because Gradle's wrapper implementation changes across versions, the most reliable solution is still:
- Generate wrapper with `gradle wrapper` and commit the produced `gradle-wrapper.jar`.
